title: Souza's Conjecture on Blowup Ramsey Numbers
statement_text: In the bound $B(G \xrightarrow{r} H; t) \leq c^t$, the exponential constant $c$ can be chosen to depend only on $H$ and $r$, and not on $G$.
context_text: Souza introduced blowup Ramsey numbers and proved $B(G \xrightarrow{r} H; t) \leq c(G,H,r)^t$. He conjectured that the dependence on $G$ in the exponential constant is unnecessary, motivated by the fact that his lower bound constant $c'$ already does not depend on $G$.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 2: $B(G \xrightarrow{r} H; t) \leq a \cdot b^t$ where $b = b(H,r)$ is independent of $G$, confirming that the exponential growth rate does not depend on $G$.
paper_title: Extremal and Ramsey results on graph blowups

title: Conjecture on unavoidable G-dependence in Theorem 2
statement_text: For some graph $H$, the dependence on $G$ in the multiplicative factor $a = a(G, H, r)$ of the bound $B(G \xrightarrow{r} H; t) \leq a \cdot b^t$ (Theorem 2) is unavoidable.
context_text: Theorem 2 shows that the exponential constant $b$ can be taken independent of $G$, but the multiplicative prefactor $a$ still depends on $G$. The authors believe this residual dependence is necessary for some $H$, and refer to the concluding remarks for further details.
notes: Stated in both the abstract and the introduction as a belief/conjecture without a labelled theorem environment; concluding remarks section was not fully captured in the PDF extraction.
paper_title: Extremal and Ramsey results on graph blowups
